5.display: Yes — the line has gradient 3, so when x increases by 1, y increases by 3; from (1, 3) to (2, 6) fits that rule.

6.display: True — e.g. when x = 2, y = 3, which is one more than x.

7.display: Costs: £3, £5, £7, £9. The graph is a straight line starting at (0, 3) with gradient 2 (cost increases by £2 per mile).

8.display: Example: (1, 2), (2, 5), (3, 8) — y increases by 3 when x increases by 1.
